book_summary: Leviticus has chapters 1-20 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The opening offering manual is framed as ancient ritual grammar for drawing near after the tabernacle is filled with glory. Chapters 8-10 narrate priestly ordination, glory, rupture, grief, and discernment. Chapters 11-15 cover clean/unclean animals, childbirth purification, scale-disease/tzaraat diagnosis and cleansing, garment/house affliction, and bodily discharges with strong anti-shame and body-honoring safeguards. Chapter 16 gives the Day of Atonement as sanctuary cleansing and communal burden-removal. Chapters 17-20 open the holiness-code material around blood/life, sanctuary-centered slaughter, sexual-boundary texts, neighbor and sojourner love, disability justice, wages, honest measures, child-sacrifice polemic, and severe penalty texts with strong anti-weaponization safeguards.

Severe holiness penalty text, Molech, spirit-practice penalties, sexual-boundary penalties, separation/land warning, anti-violence safeguards.

translation_notes: Leviticus continues Exodus: YHWH calls from the completed tent of meeting.
Korban/offering means something brought near; offerings are approach/nearness grammar, not crude appeasement.
Blood/life should be treated as ritual life-symbolism, not gore spectacle.
Clean/unclean language is ritual-state language, not body shame, moral disgust, disability stigma, or human worth ranking.
Bodies, flows, birth, illness, sex, and menstruation are not evil; washing, waiting, and offerings protect sanctuary boundaries without degrading bodies.
Leviticus 16 kippur/atonement includes cleansing sanctuary from accumulated uncleannesses and transgressions, not only individual guilt relief.
Leviticus 17 blood/life language grounds reverence for life and sanctuary-centered offering.

Leviticus 18 and 20 sexual-boundary texts are source-preserved and require deep lexical, historical, and reception-history audit. They must not be used to justify hatred, violence, bullying, criminalization, or dehumanization of LGBTQ people.
Molech/child-sacrifice polemic is life-protection and sanctuary-name protection.
Land-vomit rhetoric is covenant-land holiness language and must not be used for modern ethnic cleansing or religious violence.
Leviticus 19 is a central holiness/social ethics chapter: field margins, wages, disability justice, truth, courts, neighbor love, sojourner love, elder honor, and honest measures.
Leviticus 19:34 must be held with 19:18: love neighbor as yourself and love sojourner as yourself.
Severe penalty texts in Leviticus 20 are ancient covenant enforcement material, not modern permission for vigilante violence, state violence against identity groups, or religious abuse.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Leviticus/Vayiqra title and opening call from tent requires Torah continuity crosslink with Exodus 40.
Clean/unclean terminology requires anti-shame and ritual-state metadata across Leviticus.
Leviticus 16 kippur/atonement terminology requires sanctuary-cleansing and covering lexical audit.
Leviticus 16 Azazel requires deep source, lexical, and reception-history audit without speculative overclaiming.
Leviticus 17 blood/life and seirim/goat-powers material requires lexical and ritual-centralization audit.
Leviticus 18 sexual-boundary material requires deep source-language, ancient kinship, consent/exploitation, gender, and reception-history audit.
Leviticus 18:22 and 20:13 require dedicated lexical/context/reception audit with anti-queer-harm safeguards.
Leviticus 18/20 land-defilement and land-vomit rhetoric requires anti-colonial and anti-violence interpretation safeguards.
Leviticus 19:14 disability justice language requires anti-ableism metadata.
Leviticus 19:20 enslaved/unfree woman case requires coercion, consent, and anti-slavery audit.
Leviticus 19:28 body-marking prohibition requires ritual-identity and anti-body-shame audit.
Leviticus 19:31 and 20:6,27 spirit-practice prohibitions require reception-history safeguards and must not be used for persecution.
Leviticus 20 severe penalty material requires ancient legal-context and anti-weaponization audit.
next_actions: Continue Leviticus with chapters 21-22: priestly holiness boundaries, bodily-wholeness restrictions, priestly family status, holy food boundaries, and acceptable offerings with strong disability-justice and anti-shame safeguards.
